items.title: Easier Responsive Emails for Umbraco Forms with MJML
items.author: Søren Kottal
items.content:
HTML emails are notoriously difficult to style due to inconsistent rendering across email clients. Fortunately, MJML provides a modern solution that simplifies email creation while ensuring compatibility.

Basically, HTML emails are a Frankenstein’s monster of old web practices, because they have to work in the wild west of email clients. If they were too advanced, half of your audience wouldn't be able to read them properly.

Exhibit A: The default Umbraco Forms Email Template

Here’s an excerpt of the default email template in Umbraco Forms. While functional, it requires extensive inline styling, uses outdated table-based layouts, and lacks flexibility.

Introducing MJML: The simpler approach

Writing HTML email templates like this can be tedious and error-prone. This is where MJML comes in.

MJML (Mailjet Markup Language) is a powerful open-source framework designed to simplify the creation of responsive email templates. Instead of manually coding complex HTML tables and inline styles, MJML offers an intuitive, component-based syntax that automatically generates well-structured and mobile-friendly emails.

Instead of handwriting nested tables, and conditional Internet Explorer comments, you use easy to remember elements like , , and so forth. MJML then converts your markup to real robot-barf-like HTML tables, with all the needed weird quirks to make it work in different email clients.

I won’t go into detail about how to write MJML, you can see that in their documentation. In this article, I want to show you how you can utilize MJML for creating email templates to be used with Umbraco Forms.

First up, I’ve created an MJML implementation of the default example email template provided with Umbraco Forms. Converting it from real robot-barf-like HTML tables to clean MJML, brings it down from 221 lines of code to just 103. And with the added benefit of getting much cleaner markup, that you would be less afraid of touching. You can see the difference here.

But how would you get the MJML converted to HTML before sending the email?

Rendering MJML to HTML in .NET

Since MJML doesn’t have official .NET support, you need a library to convert MJML into standard HTML before sending the email. Fortunately, a community-driven fork of MJML is available on NuGet, implementing all MJML features and actively maintained.

Without having to create your own workflow for Umbraco Forms, to convert the MJML into HTML renderable by email clients, you can use Mjml.Net directly in the razor views used by the default Razor workflow provided with Umbraco Forms.

This would typically require you to save all your markup to a string variable, and run that through the MjmlRenderer, like this:

This converts the MJML into fully compatible HTML, which you can then send as an email.

Using MJML in Razor Views

I don’t like putting everything into a variable like this. Especially when I need to loop over form fields, and concatenate values in.

Instead of storing MJML as a string in your code, a cleaner approach is to use Razor views and the layout feature to process MJML dynamically. You can create an file like this:

Then, in your email template, simply reference the layout:

This way, the email template content is processed through the MJML renderer dynamically, allowing you to use Razor’s built-in functionality while leveraging MJML’s cleaner syntax.

By integrating MJML with Umbraco Forms, you achieve cleaner, more maintainable email templates while ensuring broad compatibility across email clients. This approach allows developers to focus on design and content rather than battling email client inconsistencies, ultimately simplifying the email creation process.

items.title: Best Practices for Structuring an Express.js Project
items.author: Mohamed Ibrahim
items.content:
How to organize your Express.js application for scalability and maintainability

Express.js is a minimalist and flexible Node.js framework, but as your project grows, a well-structured codebase becomes crucial for maintainability. In this guide, we'll cover the for structuring an Express.js project for clarity, scalability, and maintainability.

📂 Recommended Folder Structure

A clean structure keeps your project modular and scalable. Here's a commonly used Express.js project structure:

📁 my-express-app  
 ├── 📁 src  
 │   ├── 📁 config          # Configuration files (e.g., database, environment variables)  
 │   ├── 📁 controllers     # Business logic (handles requests/responses)  
 │   ├── 📁 models          # Database models & schemas  
 │   ├── 📁 routes          # API route definitions  
 │   ├── 📁 middlewares     # Custom middleware (authentication, logging, error handling)  
 │   ├── 📁 services        # Business logic or external API interactions  
 │   ├── 📁 utils           # Helper functions and utilities  
 │   ├── app.js            # Express app setup  
 │   └── server.js         # Server initialization  
 ├── .env                  # Environment variables  
 ├── .gitignore            # Files to ignore in version control  
 ├── package.json          # Dependencies and scripts  
 ├── README.md             # Project documentation

1️⃣ Separate Concerns: Use MVC Pattern

The Model-View-Controller (MVC) pattern helps organize code into logical layers:

  • → Handle database interactions
  • → Contain business logic (handling requests and responses)
  • → Define API endpoints

2️⃣ Use Environment Variables ( file)

Never hardcode sensitive information like API keys, database credentials, or JWT secrets. Instead, store them in a and load them using . 

PORT=5000
MONGO_URI=mongodb://localhost:27017/mydb
JWT_SECRET=mysecretkey

3️⃣ Use Middleware for Code Reusability

Middleware helps keep the main logic clean and reusable.

Example: Logger Middleware

4️⃣ Implement Proper Error Handling

Centralized error handling prevents redundant error-handling code.

Example: Custom Error Handler

5️⃣ Use Services for Business Logic

Keep business logic separate from controllers by using a .

6️⃣ Database Connection in a Separate File

To keep the clean, manage the database connection separately.

Example: Database Connection File

By structuring your project properly, you create a scalable, maintainable, and organized codebase that grows with your application. Following these best practices ensures your project is easy to debug, extend, and collaborate on.

💡 What structure do you use for your Express.js projects? Let me know in the comments! 🚀

📢 If you found this guide helpful, share it with fellow developers and follow me for more web development tips! You can follow me on GitHub and connect on Twitter

items.title: The Neural Network Effect: AI, Automation & the Future of Hypergrowth
items.author: DIPAK BARETH
items.content:
Introduction: The Rise of Intelligent Marketing

Imagine waking up, checking your phone, and seeing an ad for a product you didn’t even know you needed—but it’s exactly what you were looking for. No, it’s not magic. It’s AI, predictive analytics, and automation working behind the scenes to create hyper-personalized experiences.

Welcome to the Neural Network Effect—where AI and automation don’t just support marketing growth but redefine it completely. Businesses leveraging AI aren’t just scaling; they’re experiencing hypergrowth, expanding faster than ever before.

How? Let’s dive into the forces shaping this future.

1. The Neural Network Effect: Why AI Is the Ultimate Growth Engine The Neural Network Effect isn’t just a buzzword—it’s a real phenomenon. In simple terms, the more AI learns from data, the smarter and more accurate it becomes. This creates a compounding effect, where AI-driven businesses:

  • Identify high-value customers faster than ever

  • Deliver hyper-personalized marketing messages with pinpoint precision

  • Automate repetitive tasks, freeing up time for creative strategy

  • Optimize campaigns in real-time for maximum ROI

Think of it like a self-learning marketing machine—the more data it processes, the more unstoppable your growth becomes.

2. AI in Marketing: From Guesswork to Precision Targeting Old-school marketing was based on gut feelings and broad demographics. Today, AI eliminates the guesswork.

Here’s how AI is transforming marketing from mass targeting to laser-focused precision:

  • – AI analyzes past behavior to anticipate customer needs before they even search for a product.

  • – Algorithms detect emotions in social media conversations to refine messaging and engagement.

  • Hyper-Personalized Content – AI tools like OpenAI’s GPT models craft content tailored to each individual, increasing engagement rates.

  • – AI automates real-time ad bidding, maximizing ad spend efficiency.

: Netflix doesn’t show you a random homepage—it curates recommendations based on your viewing habits. That’s AI-powered marketing at work.

3. Automation: The Secret to Scaling Without Limits Marketing hypergrowth isn’t just about being fast—it’s about being efficient. Automation allows brands to scale without adding extra workload.

  • – Handle 24/7 customer queries, increasing conversions effortlessly.

  • Automated Email Sequences – AI personalizes email content based on user actions, boosting engagement.

  • – Predict which leads are most likely to convert, so sales teams focus on the right people.

  • AI-Powered Social Media Scheduling – Tools like Hootsuite and Buffer use AI to post at optimal times for engagement.

Businesses using AI-driven automation see higher efficiency, reduced costs, and faster growth—without burning out their teams.

4. The Future: AI’s Role in Predictive & Autonomous Marketing The next phase of AI in marketing isn’t just reactive—it’s proactive and autonomous. Imagine:

  • AI running entire campaigns without human input

  • Voice search optimizing content automatically

  • Virtual AI sales reps handling personalized customer interactions

Soon, businesses won’t just be using AI—they’ll be competing on whose AI is smarter. The companies that adapt early will dominate.

5. Final Thoughts: Adapt or Be Left Behind The Neural Network Effect is already reshaping marketing. AI is getting smarter, automation is eliminating bottlenecks, and the brands leveraging these tools aren’t just growing—they’re scaling at unprecedented rates.

So, the question isn’t whether AI will change marketing—it already has. The real question is:

Will you ride the wave, or will you be left behind?

How are you using AI in your marketing strategy? Drop a comment below!

items.title: Laravel 12 A Beginner-Friendly Guide
items.author: Robin 🎭
items.content:
Laravel is one of the most popular PHP frameworks used by developers around the world. It is known for its simplicity, elegance, and ease of use. With each new release, Laravel continues to improve, making it even easier to build powerful and scalable web applications. Now, Laravel 12 has arrived, bringing new improvements and optimizations while maintaining its core philosophy of simplicity and efficiency.

In this guide, we will explore Laravel 12 in detail. Whether you are a beginner or an experienced developer, this article will help you understand the latest version and how it can benefit your development projects.

Before we dive into Laravel 12, let’s first understand what Laravel is. Laravel is an open-source PHP framework that follows the Model-View-Controller (MVC) architecture. It provides tools and features to simplify web application development, including authentication, routing, session management, and database handling.

Laravel is designed to make coding more enjoyable and less repetitive by providing built-in tools that eliminate the need to write repetitive code. This makes it a preferred choice for developers who want to build high-quality applications quickly.

Laravel has gained popularity because of several reasons:

  • – Laravel provides clear documentation and an intuitive syntax, making it beginner-friendly.
  • – It comes with built-in features like authentication, routing, and caching, reducing development time.
  • – Laravel has a strong community of developers who continuously contribute to its growth and improvement.
  • – It includes built-in security features to protect applications from common vulnerabilities.

What’s New in Laravel 12?

Laravel 12 continues to build on the strong foundation of previous versions. It introduces improvements in performance, ease of use, and compatibility with modern development tools. Here are some highlights:

  • Improved performance and speed
  • Better support for frontend frameworks like React and Vue
  • New tools to make development faster
  • More efficient database handling

Getting started with Laravel 12 is easy. Follow these steps to install Laravel on your system:

Laravel requires Composer, a dependency manager for PHP. You can download and install Composer from getcomposer.org.

Step 2: Create a New Laravel Project

Once Composer is installed, run the following command to create a new Laravel project:

composer create-project  laravel/laravel myproject

Replace with the name of your application.

Step 3: Start the Development Server

Navigate to your project folder and start the Laravel development server using:

myproject
php artisan serve

You should now be able to access your Laravel application in a web browser at .

Understanding Laravel 12 Basics

When you open your Laravel project folder, you will see several directories. Here’s a quick overview:

  • – Contains the core application code.
  • – Stores configuration files.
  • – Includes migration and seed files.
  • – Holds publicly accessible files.
  • – Defines the application's routes.
  • – Contains views, CSS, and JavaScript files.

Routing is how Laravel directs web requests to the correct part of your application. Laravel 12 makes routing even more efficient. Routes are defined in for web applications.

This means when a user visits the home page (), Laravel will show the file from the directory.

3. Blade Templating Engine

Blade is Laravel’s powerful templating engine that makes writing HTML easier. Instead of writing complex PHP code inside HTML, Blade uses simple syntax.

My Laravel AppWelcome, {{ $name }}!

In a controller, you can pass data to this view like this:

Migrations in Laravel allow you to manage database tables using PHP instead of SQL.

To create a new table, run:

php artisan make:migration create_users_table

Then, update the generated file in to define the table structure.

Eloquent is Laravel’s built-in ORM (Object-Relational Mapping) tool that simplifies database interactions.

This saves a new user to the database without writing raw SQL queries.

Managed or Self-Hosted Deployment Platforms

Laravel 12 provides flexibility when it comes to deployment. Developers can choose between or solutions.

  • These include Laravel Forge, Envoyer, and cloud services like AWS, DigitalOcean, and Heroku. These services handle server setup, scaling, and security automatically.
  • Developers can deploy Laravel applications on their own servers, such as using Apache or Nginx, with manual configuration and maintenance.

Choosing between managed and self-hosted deployment depends on factors like budget, expertise, and project requirements.

Laravel 12 is a powerful and efficient framework for building modern web applications. Whether you are a beginner or an expert, Laravel provides tools and features that make development easier.

With improved speed, better frontend support, and enhanced database management, Laravel 12 is a great choice for anyone looking to build robust web applications.

items.title: 📚 Understanding Closures in JavaScript: A Complete Guide
items.author: Artem Turlenko
items.content:
Closures are one of the most powerful and sometimes confusing concepts in JavaScript. They are frequently asked about in interviews and are essential for writing efficient, modular, and secure code. In this post, we’ll break down , , and for them.

A is a function that retains access to its , even when the function is executed outside that scope.

Outer: Hello, Inner: World
  • retains access to even after has finished executing.
  • This behavior is what we call a .

Closures work because of . When a function is defined, it remembers the variables in its surrounding scope. Even if the outer function is no longer active in the execution stack, the inner function keeps a reference to those variables.

JavaScript manages closures using the and the . The inner function doesn’t just have access to its own local variables, but also variables from its parent function and the global scope.

⚡ Practical Use Cases of Closures

1️⃣ Data Privacy (Encapsulation)

Closures allow creating private variables, preventing direct access from outside.

Here, is private and cannot be accessed directly.

Closures allow functions to be dynamically created based on an input value.

This is useful for and .

3️⃣ Event Listeners with State

Closures help maintain state in event handlers.

Each time the button is clicked, the event listener retains access to .

4️⃣ Memoization (Performance Optimization)

Closures help cache results and improve performance.

Closures store previously computed values, reducing redundant calculations.

  • Closures allow functions to retain access to their outer scope even after execution.
  • They are created automatically when functions are returned from other functions.
  • Useful for , , , and performance optimizations.

Closures might seem tricky at first, but once you understand their behavior, they become an incredibly powerful tool in JavaScript development.

💬 Have you used closures in your projects? Share your experiences in the comments! 🚀

items.title: MEASURES VS CALCULATED COLUMNS IN POWER BI
items.author: Katie
items.content:
In my beginner era as a Power Bi expert, differentiating between a calculated column and a measure, and when to use them was a major challenge. The two are important aspects in Power BI and understanding when and where to use them is key. This article will delve into the two concepts and explain the circumstances where we should use a calculated column or a measure.

A Measure is a calculation created using DAX (Data Analysis Expressions) that is performed on the fly as you interact with your reports in Power BI. Measures are used in visuals, pivot tables, and pivot charts. Unlike Calculated Columns, the calculation is not pre-computed. Putting it in simpler terms, measures are efficient for aggregated calculations.

Calculated Columns in Power BI

A Calculated Column is a column that you add to an existing table in the data model in Power BI. It’s computed during the data loading phase, and the values are stored in the Power BI model. The formula for a Calculated Column is calculated for each row in a table.

Measure Vs Calculated Column

items.title: Micro Frontends Architecture
items.author: Drishti Saraf
items.content:
In modern web development, applications are becoming larger and more complex. While Microservices have revolutionized backend development, Micro Frontends bring similar advantages to the frontend world. This approach allows independent teams to develop, test, and deploy different parts of a web application separately, leading to better scalability and maintainability.

In this blog, we will explore what Micro Frontends are, why they are useful, how they work, and how to implement them in a project.

What is Micro Frontends Architecture?

Micro Frontends is an architectural pattern where a web application is broken down into smaller, independent frontend components or applications that can be developed, deployed, and managed separately. These micro applications are then composed together to create a seamless user experience.

  • Different teams can work on different frontend components.

  • Different parts of the frontend can be built using different frameworks (React, Angular, Vue, etc.).

  • Decentralized Deployment: Individual components can be updated and deployed without affecting the entire application.

  • Enhances scalability by allowing independent teams to manage different parts of the frontend.

Understanding Micro Frontends with an Example

Consider a company that provides multiple applications to its customers:

  1. Used for managing business processes and resources.

  2. E-commerce Admin Dashboard: Used for managing products, orders, and customers.

  3. License Management System: Used for managing software licenses.

A single customer may use all three applications, and they share a single login application that handles authentication and user management.

Using a Micro Frontends Architecture, each of these applications can be developed independently while still being seamlessly integrated into a single unified experience.

How Micro Frontends Help in This Scenario:

  • Each system (ERP, E-commerce Admin, License Management) can be updated separately without affecting others.

  • The login application can be a standalone Micro Frontend, shared across all systems.

  • Different teams can use different frontend frameworks suitable for their needs.

  • New applications can be added without disrupting existing ones.

For instance, the login application could be built with React, while the ERP dashboard might use Angular, and the License Management system might be developed in Vue.js. With Micro Frontends, these different frontend technologies can co-exist within the same ecosystem while still functioning as a cohesive application.

Challenges and Best Practices

  1. - Managing state across multiple frontend modules can be complex.

  2. - Coordinating navigation between different Micro Frontends requires careful planning.

  3. - Different Micro Frontends may have conflicting styles, which needs to be handled properly (e.g., using CSS Modules or Shadow DOM).

  • Use Module Federation for seamless integration.

  • Implement Shared State Management using Redux, Context API, or Event Bus.

  • Adopt Consistent Design Guidelines to maintain uniformity across Micro Frontends.

  • Utilize CI/CD Pipelines for automated deployment of individual Micro Frontends.

Micro Frontends Architecture is a game-changer for scalable and modular frontend applications. It enables independent development, faster deployments, and the freedom to use different technologies within the same project. By leveraging Module Federation, Single SPA, or Web Components, you can create maintainable and efficient frontend applications.

items.title: Why Developers Struggle to Make Money with Apps (And How to Fix It)
items.author: Lin Wang
items.content:
Developers love building things. We get excited about new ideas, start coding right away, and create something we think is amazing. But then… nothing happens.

📉 No downloads. No revenue. Just silence.

Why? Because we often forget to ask the most important question—Does anyone actually need this app?

Why Market Research Matters More Than Your Code

❌ Most developers build first and validate later. That’s a mistake.

Here’s how to avoid wasting months on an app no one wants:

Look at similar apps. If an app in your niche is making money, that’s a strong sign of demand. If there are zero competitors, be careful—maybe there’s no market.

✅ Read user reviews on the App Store.

1-2 star reviews = complaints & frustrations. 4-star reviews = users love the app but want improvements.

Find gaps and build a better alternative.

Use Sensor Tower or AppTweak to see if people are searching for solutions like yours. If search volume is low, validate on Reddit, Google Trends, or Facebook groups before building.

The Mistake I Made (And What I Learned)

I once spent a whole month building an app that I thought was genius. Launched it… and nothing happened.

🚀 Not because the app was bad—but because nobody needed it.

💡 Don’t make the same mistake. Before writing a single line of code, validate your idea.

items.title: How to Hard Refresh in Edge
items.author: Bhawana
items.content:
It may have happened to all of us at some point where the webpage in Edge didn’t load correctly, failed to load, or showed old content even though you have made new changes. This is exactly where you need to hard refresh in Edge to ensure the latest content appears.

Steps to Hard Refresh in Edge Using Keyboard Shortcuts

Performing a hard refresh in Edge is simple and can be done using keyboard shortcuts, which vary slightly by operating system.

Hard Refresh Edge on Windows and Linux:

The keyboard shortcuts to hard refresh Edge on Windows and Linux are:

  • Commonly used to bypass the cache and reload the webpage.

  • An alternative shortcut that also clears the cache for the page and reloads the latest content.

Edge will reload the page, fetching the latest version directly from the server and ignoring cached files.

Hard Refresh Edge on macOS:

MacOS users have different shortcuts for hard-refreshing Edge:

  • The primary shortcut to clear the page cache and load the newest version of the website.

  • An alternative that also ensures the cache is bypassed.

After performing a hard refresh, you should see the most up-to-date content for the page, as it bypasses the local cache.

Steps to Clear Cache and Hard Refresh in Edge Manually

Sometimes, a simple hard refresh might not resolve certain issues, especially if the browser’s cache has accumulated a lot of data over time. In these cases, it’s helpful to manually clear the cache and then perform a hard refresh on Edge.

  1. Right-click the menu at the top of the browser window.

  2. Select from the dropdown.

  1. Click Privacy, search, and services in the left-hand sidebar.

  2. Scroll down to the .

  3. Select the items you want to delete by clicking the button.

  1. In the dialog box, make sure that are checked. You can also clear other browsing data if necessary (e.g., cookies, history), but this is optional.

  2. Once you have selected the data to clear, click the button to remove the cached files from Edge.

After clearing the cache, revisit the webpage and use the hard refresh Edge keyboard shortcuts discussed earlier (e.g., Ctrl + F5 or Command + Shift + R) to ensure the latest version of the site loads without any cached files.

Other Ways to Hard Refresh in Edge

In addition to using keyboard shortcuts and manually clearing the cache, there are a few other ways you can hard refresh your Edge browser.

The Developer Tools in Edge provide another method for performing a hard refresh. This is particularly useful for web developers who need to clear cache, cookies, and more during testing.

  1. Open Microsoft Edge and press either to access the Developer Tools panel.

  2. In the Developer Tools interface, right-click the button in the Edge toolbar.

  1. Select and from the menu.

This will clear the cache for the page you’re on and reload it with the latest files from the server.

Another way to hard refresh in Edge involves the use of browser extensions. Numerous extensions are available that help users clear cache and hard refresh pages with just one single click. These extensions are useful if you often need to refresh pages without having to use shortcuts.

One such extension is “Clear Cache,” which allows you to clear cached data for specific pages and automatically reload them afterward.

Troubleshooting Hard Refresh Issues in Microsoft Edge

Sometimes, despite performing a hard refresh in Edge, users may still experience issues such as:

  • The webpage is not showing the most recent content.

  • Layout or design problems continue to persist even after refreshing.

  • Some features on the page are not functioning properly as expected.

Possible Causes and Solutions:

  • Some browser extensions can interfere with the cache-clearing process. Try disabling all extensions and perform a hard refresh again to see if the issue is resolved.

  • If cookies are causing issues with the page, clearing the cookies along with the cache might help. Go to Edge’s Clear browsing data menu and select Cookies and other site data.

  • If you’re still having trouble, the issue could be with your local DNS cache. To clear the DNS cache on your system:

  • On Windows, open the command prompt and type .

  • On macOS, open the terminal and type sudo killall -HUP mDNSResponder.

  • In some cases, incorrect browser settings can lead to problems with refreshing. Restoring Edge’s default settings or reinstalling the browser might help resolve any persistent issues.

Getting a hard refresh in Edge is a great way to make sure you’re seeing the newest stuff on a website. Whether you’re dealing with old pages, problems with how things look or need to fix some web pages, knowing how to do a hard refresh can really come in handy.

By getting the hang of when and how to hard refresh in Edge, you can make your browsing time better and make sure you’re always seeing the latest updates.

items.title: How to Maximize Your Forex API Without Hitting Limits
items.author: Brandon
items.content:
If you're a trader, developer, or financial analyst, using a is essential for accessing real-time currency data. But have you ever been frustrated by ? These restrictions can disrupt trading strategies, slow down application performance, and create unnecessary headaches.

Fortunately, there are ways to while avoiding limits. In this guide, we'll cover smart strategies to make the most out of your Forex API without running into restrictions. Plus, we’ll link to an in-depth guide on for additional tips and best practices.

Why Do Forex APIs Have Usage Limits?

Before diving into solutions, it’s important to understand why API providers set limits:

  • – Too many requests can slow down servers.
  • – Ensures all users get equal access to resources.
  • – Many providers offer paid plans with higher limits.
  • – Data processing and distribution come with expenses.

By recognizing these factors, you can take steps to your API calls and avoid unnecessary limitations.

Smart Strategies to Avoid Hitting API Limits

1. Upgrade to a Suitable API Plan

If you’re frequently exceeding limits, consider upgrading to a . Many Forex API providers, including ForexRatesAPI, offer different pricing models based on usage needs.

2. Use Caching to Reduce API Calls

Instead of requesting fresh data every second, store recent API responses in a . This is especially useful if:

  • You’re displaying forex rates on a website.
  • You need to access the same data multiple times within a short period.
  • You’re running a bot or algorithmic trading strategy that checks prices frequently.

3. Batch Requests to Minimize API Calls

Rather than making multiple small requests, whenever possible. Some APIs allow you to retrieve multiple currency pairs or historical data in a single call, reducing the overall number of requests you send.

4. Schedule API Calls During Low-Traffic Hours

Some APIs enforce stricter limits during peak trading hours. If you’re not actively trading, schedule your requests during off-peak times, such as:

  • Early mornings before major markets open.
  • Weekends when forex trading is closed.

5. Use Webhooks Instead of Polling

Rather than constantly checking for new forex data, use to receive updates only when rates change. This dramatically reduces unnecessary API requests and helps you stay within limits.

Most providers offer an where you can track your request limits. Regularly checking your usage can help you:

  • Avoid unexpected interruptions.
  • Identify unnecessary API calls.
  • Plan for a higher-tier subscription if needed.

Forex APIs are powerful tools, but can be a major inconvenience. By optimizing your requests, caching data, scheduling API calls wisely, and upgrading your plan when necessary, you can get the most out of your Forex Rates API without disruption.

1. What happens if I exceed my API limit?

Your requests may be throttled, delayed, or even blocked until your limit resets.

2. How can I check my API usage?

Most API providers offer dashboards where you can monitor request counts and remaining limits.

3. Can I request higher API limits?

Yes! Many providers offer custom enterprise plans with based on your needs.

4. Is there a way to bypass API limits for free?

While you can optimize requests using caching and batching, exceeding free limits usually requires upgrading to a paid plan.

5. Does ForexRatesAPI offer unlimited API calls?

ForexRatesAPI provides scalable plans, allowing you to based on your data needs. Check out the available plans on forexratesapi.com.

items.author: Sk
items.content:
Because why not? Software is fun.

I know this sounds insane; assembly for fun—but trust me, you feel alive.

I’m lucky to be at a point where I can build almost anything, so I’m testing the waters for a low-level series combining Node.js and assembly as a shared library.

In this article, we’re saying hello to the world in x86 Linux assembly.

You need a Linux environment. On Windows, you can use WSL2.

Create a file called and add the following code:

Compile the program using . Installing it on Linux is simple—just install the GNU tools with:

apt update apt  build-essential gdb libgtk-3-dev

Then compile your assembly file:

This command creates the object file. Next, link it:

Finally, run your program:

This might be the most complex "Hello World" you’ve ever seen, especially if you’re new to assembly.

Assembly programs are divided into sections:

  • → Where we store data, like "Hello, World!"
  • → Where we write instructions
  • → Defines the program’s entry point

Symbols are names for memory locations.

For example, is a symbol. It tells assembly, "Hey, remember this location—we’ll use it later." It’s like a variable.

When you add a , it becomes a label, defining the value of a symbol.

This means: "At memory location , store the string 'Hello, World!\n'."

This pattern is everywhere. is also a label:

Making global lets the assembler and linker find it.

Assembly doesn’t track memory for you—you have to do it manually.

is just an address. It points to the start of , but it doesn’t know where it ends.

is the current memory location—right after the string.

So, means:

Take the address after and subtract the start address.

That gives us the string’s length, which we need for the call.

Registers store data temporarily and operate way faster than RAM.

In our program, is a register.

But before we can execute anything, we need to talk about...

The kernel is the middleman between your program and hardware. Every system call (file access, memory allocation, exiting a program) goes through the kernel.

You’re asking the kernel for help.

But the kernel has rules. Before calling it, you must set up registers with the correct values.

For example, to a program, the kernel expects:

  • → "I want to exit."
  • → "Exited successfully."

It’s the same as returning 0 in C:

Unlike , writing to the console requires more information:

  1. → The number 4 tells the kernel, "I want to write."
  2. → The number 1 means "write to stdout."
  3. → Where’s the message? It’s at .
  4. → How long is the message? That’s in .
  5. → Call the kernel to execute the write.

Once we’ve written our message, we :

This program uses , which is common in GNU assemblers.

Intel syntax, used in NASM, looks slightly different. A major difference is that AT&T syntax prefixes registers with .

Some people prefer Intel syntax, but most books stick with AT&T, so it’s worth learning.

That’s it! You just wrote and understood x86 assembly’s "Hello, World!"

items.title: What You Should Know About HTTP/HTTPS Proxy
items.author: Swiftproxy - Residential Proxies
items.content:
Proxy confusion is real. HTTP, HTTPS, SOCKS… the list goes on. But here's the deal: using the wrong proxy can leave you exposed to security breaches or compatibility headaches. Whether you’re securing sensitive data or scraping content, understanding when to use HTTP vs. HTTPS proxies is crucial. In this post, I’ll help you clear the fog. No jargon, no fluff—just clear, actionable insights you can use right now.

Let's cut to the chase: an HTTP proxy is an intermediary. It stands between you and the internet. Here’s how it works: 1.You make a web request.

2.Instead of going directly to the site, it’s sent to the proxy server.

3.The proxy then forwards your request to the website, masking your IP in the process.

4.The website responds, and the proxy sends that back to you.

It sounds like a lot, but the good news? With modern proxies, this process happens in milliseconds.

Different Types of HTTP Proxies

Not all proxies are the same. There are three main types of HTTP proxies, and they vary based on the level of anonymity they offer. Used by schools, businesses, and governments, transparent proxies control internet access. Think of them as digital gatekeepers.

But they don’t hide your IP. Websites still know who you are. These proxies are more about limiting access than protecting your privacy.

Looking to mask your IP? That’s where an anonymous proxy comes in. It replaces your real IP with one of its own. But there's a catch—these proxies often identify themselves as proxies to the websites they interact with. That means streaming sites like Netflix might block you.

If you're trying to access content outside of your region, an anonymous proxy won’t always do the trick.

Now we’re talking. Elite proxies don’t just hide your IP—they go further. They won’t reveal themselves as proxies to the websites they connect with. Your browsing is effectively anonymous.

However, these proxies work well for anonymity, but they're not ideal when security is the priority. They still use the HTTP protocol, which lacks the "S" (Secure) that HTTPS offers.

The Distinctions Between HTTP and HTTPS Proxy

The distinction between HTTP and HTTPS proxies is simple but critical: security. It’s faster and cheaper. Great for non-sensitive tasks like browsing or scraping. But there's no encryption, so your data isn’t secure. This is where security comes into play. HTTPS proxies encrypt the data between your device and the proxy server, ensuring end-to-end security. Ideal for tasks like online banking or handling sensitive transactions.

Which Proxy Is Right for You

Your choice depends entirely on your needs. Let’s break it down:HTTP Proxies Are Great For: Access geo-blocked videos or music. Perfect for pulling large amounts of data, like product information or reviews. Track competitors or market trends without giving away your identity. If you're automating repetitive tasks, HTTP proxies won’t slow you down.HTTPS Proxies Are Ideal For: If security matters—use HTTPS proxies for anonymous browsing. When you’re dealing with sensitive financial data, like credit card info or banking. These proxies mask your digital footprint, helping you sidestep trackers and cookies.

The Advantage of HTTPS Proxies

HTTP proxies have been around for years, and they’re still great for low-risk tasks like streaming. But as security becomes more important, HTTPS proxies are the way to go—especially if you're working with sensitive data.

Whether you're looking to scrape data at scale or securely complete transactions, your choice of proxy matters. With the fastest and most secure residential proxy network available, you can ensure smooth and safe online operations.

items.title: Driving Innovation with Data: Certified Full Stack Data Scientist
items.author: Maria Mathur
items.content:
In the data-driven era of today, organizations make decisions, streamline processes, and gain a competitive advantage based on data. A Certified Full Stack Data Scientist bridges the gap between raw data and actionable insights. With skills covering data engineering, analysis, machine learning, and deployment, these experts drive innovation by converting intricate datasets into business solutions of value. This article discusses the primary skills, technologies, and roles of a full-stack data scientist.

Most Important Features of a Full Stack Data Scientist

Data Collection & Engineering

Handling data sources, APIs, and web scraping methods. Dealing with databases like SQL, NoSQL, and cloud storage.

Data cleaning, processing, and structuring raw data into organized forms.

Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) & Visualization

Utilizing statistical techniques to gain insights into data distribution and trends. Utilizing visualization tools such as Matplotlib, Seaborn, Tableau, and Power BI.

Detecting correlations, outliers, and trends to make informed decisions.

Machine Learning & AI Implementation

Constructing predictive models with supervised and unsupervised learning methods. Utilizing deep learning platforms such as TensorFlow and PyTorch.

Tuning algorithms for accuracy and scalability.

A Certified Full Stack Data Scientist needs to possess a mix of technical skill, analytical reasoning, and problem-solving skills. These experts are the key to data-driven innovation, enabling enterprises to maximize performance, forecast upcoming trends, and make data-driven decisions. As data continues to influence industries, becoming a master in full-stack data science will be a priceless and sought-after skillset in the future.

items.title: VicRoads Launches Passkeys with Corbado
items.author: vdelitz
items.content:
Corbado & VicRoads bring passkeys to almost 5 million Australians

Melbourne, February 27, 2025 – Almost 5 million Victorians can now log into their myVicRoads accounts using passkeys, marking a major milestone for digital identity in Australia. By partnering with passkeys specialist Corbado, VicRoads joins the ranks of major global organizations such as Google, Amazon and PayPal in adopting passkeys as a secure, passwordless authentication method.

As one of the first government agencies in Australia to implement passkeys, VicRoads sets a new standard for secure and user-friendly digital services.

Why VicRoads decided to implement passkeys

By replacing passwords with , VicRoads reduces the risk of phishing and other online threats.

With this implementation, VicRoads provides a customer-centric login experience where customers authenticate with their fingerprint, facial recognition, a PIN, or swipe pattern, making logins more secure and seamless.

“Digital security is a key priority for VicRoads, and passkeys reflect our dedication to keeping our 4.8m myVicRoads customers’ information safe and delivering a secure and seamless user experience,” said VicRoads Chief Technology Officer, Crispin Blackall.“Our partnership with Corbado is already proving highly successful, with over 200,000 Passkeys created during our recent pilot,” he said.

VicRoads leads the way in government digital innovation

VicRoads joins Telstra, myGov and UBank as one of the first organizations in Australia to introduce passkeys, positioning itself as a digital leader in public services.

To drive , VicRoads is actively encouraging users to transition from existing authentication methods to passkeys as the preferred login method.

“By going passkey-first, VicRoads is providing its customers with both the highest level of security and the best user experience.” said Vincent Delitz, Managing Director at Corbado.

VicRoads implements passkeys with Corbado, following FIDO industry standards

The passkey implementation was carried out in collaboration with passkey specialist Corbado, a FIDO Alliance member, which provided the underlying technology and guided VicRoads on best practices for implementation and user adoption.

"Passkeys represent a paradigm shift in how we authenticate users to digital identity services," remarked Andrew Shikiar, Chief Executive Officer of the FIDO Alliance.

"VicRoads' adoption of passkeys showcases how government agencies can leverage this industry-wide innovation to protect citizen data while simplifying access to critical services. This is a significant step towards a more secure and efficient digital future for Victoria and beyond."

By rolling out passkeys at scale, VicRoads is leading the way for other government agencies and high-security industries looking to modernize authentication without compromising on security or usability.

Corbado is a leading provider of passkeys-as-a-service, helping large-scale enterprises and government agencies deploy passkey authentication seamlessly. As a FIDO Alliance member, Corbado specializes in high-adoption passkey rollouts that integrate into existing identity systems without user migration.

VicRoads registers more than 6 million vehicles annually and licenses more than 5 million drivers. VicRoads Registration & Licensing operates as a joint venture partnership with the Victorian State Government, Aware Super, Australian Retirement Trust and Macquarie Asset Management.
